notes: Announced May 1997; shipped November 1997. HP 9000 line discontinued 2003; succeeded by HP Integrity (Itanium).
15.3 GB/sec crossbar; derived from Convex supercomputing tech; operated at 120 MHz over 64-bit path.
PA-8200 in initial V2200; PA-8500 in 1998 upgrade. PA-RISC succeeded by Itanium (IA-64) in 2001.
HP planned NUMA extension for 1999 V-Class beyond 32-way SMP. NUMA became standard in modern multi-socket servers.
HP anticipated Merced integration for 1999 V-Class. Merced (Itanium) shipped 2001, not 1999. Itanium ultimately failed to displace x86-64; discontinued 2021.
Hybrid SMP/MPP cluster; EPS40 = up to 16 V2200 nodes (256 processors). Discontinued with HP 9000 line.
V2200 required HP-UX 11.0 (released November 1997). HP-UX still supported on HP Integrity but no new development.
Decision support benchmark cited for competitive comparisons. Superseded by TPC-H.
Aberdeen dismissed NT for enterprise production workloads in 1997. NT evolved to Windows 2000/Server 2003 which gained significant enterprise traction.
